Contents

01 - The thesis 02 - Founding & operating core 03 - The agentic operating layer 04 - Advisors 05 - Where the team has built 06 - How we govern agents

01 - The thesis: a team built the way the product is built

QINV is an AI-native investment operating system. The team is the first proof of the thesis: a small, senior human core that designs, supervises, and governs a large fleet of agents, the same operating pattern we sell to private banks and asset managers.

Senior by design
The human layer is concentrated in founders and specialists with institutional finance and large-scale engineering backgrounds, not a junior pyramid.
Agents do the volume
Repeatable execution across code, research, product, and operations runs as supervised agent workflows, with humans owning judgment and accountability.

Advisors and specialists are augmented, never replaced. Every agent workflow has a named human owner accountable for outcomes, controls, and sign-off.

03 - The agentic operating layer

The human core orchestrates 150+ agents organized by domain. Each domain has a human owner; agents handle volume and repeatability under human review.

150+Agents in production across domains
11Humans in the operating layer
5Domains under named human owners
24/7Continuous supervised execution
DomainFocusAgent fleetWhat runs as supervised agents
Engineering & PlatformArchitecture, infra, DevOps~40 agentsCI/CD, code generation, test synthesis, observability, and incident triage run as supervised agent pipelines.
Quant & ResearchModels, backtesting, signals~35 agentsStrategy research, data ingestion, factor analysis, and validation loops executed by orchestrated agent fleets.
Product & DesignUX, advisory workflows~25 agentsSpec drafting, UI scaffolding, content, and QA automated under human product direction.
Go-to-Market & OpsSales, onboarding, support~30 agentsProposal assembly, client onboarding playbooks, and support triage handled by agent workflows.
Risk, Compliance & Web3Controls, custody, on-chain~20 agentsPolicy checks, audit-trail synthesis, and on-chain monitoring supervised by domain owners.

Agent counts are indicative of current operating scale and evolve continuously. The governing principle is fixed: humans own judgment, agents own volume.

06 - How we govern the agent fleet

Named ownership
Every domain and every critical workflow has an accountable human owner. No agent ships to production unsupervised.
Human-in-the-loop on judgment
Decisions with client, capital, or compliance impact require human approval against defined thresholds.
Auditability by default
Agent actions are logged and reviewable, the same audit-trail discipline we deliver in the product.
Scales without a junior pyramid
Output grows by adding governed agent capacity, not by adding headcount, preserving senior judgment density.
